examples.title: Linear Search Algorithm
examples.description: Algorithm to search for an element in an unsorted array using linear search
examples.code:
PROCEDURE LinearSearch(arr, n, key)
  FOR i ← 0 TO n-1 DO
    IF arr[i] = key THEN
      RETURN i
    END IF
  END FOR
  RETURN -1
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: The algorithm iterates through each element of the array sequentially and compares it with the key. If found, it returns the index; otherwise returns -1.

examples.title: Binary Search Algorithm
examples.description: Algorithm to search for an element in a sorted array using binary search
examples.code:
PROCEDURE BinarySearch(arr, low, high, key)
  WHILE low ≤ high DO
    mid ← (low + high) / 2
    IF arr[mid] = key THEN
      RETURN mid
    ELSE IF arr[mid] < key THEN
      low ← mid + 1
    ELSE
      high ← mid - 1
    END IF
  END WHILE
  RETURN -1
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: Binary search divides the sorted array into half each iteration. It compares the middle element with the key and eliminates the half where the key cannot exist.

examples.title: Array Insertion in Python
examples.description: Python code to insert an element at a specific position in a list (array)
examples.code:
def insert_element(arr, position, value):
    if len(arr) == max_size:
        print("Array is full!")
        return arr
    if position < 0 or position > len(arr):
        print("Invalid position!")
        return arr
    
    # Shift elements to the right
    for i in range(len(arr), position, -1):
        arr[i] = arr[i-1]
    
    arr[position] = value
    return arr

# Example usage
arr = [10, 20, 30, 40, 50]
max_size = 10
arr = insert_element(arr, 2, 25)
print(arr)  # Output: [10, 20, 25, 30, 40, 50]
examples.explanation: This Python function inserts an element at a specified position by shifting all elements from that position to the right by one place, then placing the new element.

examples.title: Array Deletion in C
examples.description: C program to delete an element from an array at a specified position
examples.explanation: This C program deletes an element by shifting all elements after the deleted position one place to the left, effectively overwriting the element to be deleted.

examples.title: Bubble Sort Algorithm
examples.description: Algorithm to sort an array using bubble sort technique
examples.code:
PROCEDURE BubbleSort(arr, n)
  FOR i ← 0 TO n-2 DO
    swapped ← FALSE
    FOR j ← 0 TO n-i-2 DO
      IF arr[j] > arr[j+1] THEN
        temp ← arr[j]
        arr[j] ← arr[j+1]
        arr[j+1] ← temp
        swapped ← TRUE
      END IF
    END FOR
    IF swapped = FALSE THEN
      BREAK
    END IF
  END FOR
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: Bubble sort repeatedly swaps adjacent elements if they are in wrong order. After each pass, the largest unsorted element 'bubbles up' to its correct position.

examples.title: 2D Array Address Calculation in Java
examples.description: Java program to calculate address of an element in a 2D array stored in row-major order
examples.explanation: This Java program demonstrates the address calculation formula for 2D arrays in row-major order. The formula is Base + ((row × numberOfColumns) + column) × elementSize.
